name: Ethiopia PHC Hybrid CDSS Baseline
sources: Ethiopian Primary Health Care Clinical Guideline
WHO emergency and referral red-flag guidance
General internal medicine danger-sign and referral guidance

hardStopRules.message: Critical hypoxia detected (SpO2 <= 90%). Urgent escalation/referral is required.
Fever with neck stiffness suggests possible severe CNS infection. Urgent referral is required.
Maternal severe hypertension with danger symptoms detected. Emergency referral is required.
Pregnancy-related bleeding is a maternal emergency red flag. Urgent referral is required.
Pediatric respiratory danger signs detected. Urgent referral is required.
Pediatric weight is required before final treatment/disposition decisions.
Diagnosis is required before admit/discharge decisions.
Outstanding orders exist. Confirm acknowledgment before discharge.

warningRules.message: Some required vitals are missing for this checkpoint.
Discharge with pending orders acknowledged. Ensure patient follow-up instructions are clear.

adult: Use local PHC first-line management and reassess response at follow-up.
Escalate/referral if red flags persist or worsen.
pediatric: Use age/weight-adjusted management and reassess early.
If respiratory distress or danger signs progress, refer urgently.
maternal: Apply maternal danger-sign screening at every encounter.
Escalate urgently for severe BP, bleeding, severe headache, visual symptoms, or epigastric pain.
